perturbation.original_text: NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the foregoing premises and the mutual covenants and agreements contained herein, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the parties hereto, intending to be legally bound hereby, agree as follows: 1. Board Composition Matters. (a) Appointment of New Director A. The Company agrees that it shall take all action as is necessary (including, without limitation, calling a special meeting of the Board to approve all actions contemplated hereby), effective immediately following the execution of this Agreement, to (i) cause the Board to increase the size of its membership from six (6) to seven (7) members; (ii) accept the resignation of one existing member of the Board to become effective immediately prior to the appointment of New Director A; and (iii) appoint New Director A to the Board with a term on the Board expiring at the Company's 2018 Annual Meeting of Stockholders (the "2018 Annual Meeting") and until his successor is duly elected and qualified.
perturbation.changed_text: NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the foregoing premises and the mutual covenants and agreements contained herein, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the parties hereto, intending to be legally bound hereby, agree as follows: 1. Board Composition Matters. (a) Appointment of New Director A. The Organization agrees that it shall take all action as is necessary (including, without limitation, calling a special meeting of the Board to approve all actions contemplated hereby), effective immediately following the execution of this Agreement, to (i) cause the Board to increase the size of its membership from six (6) to seven (7) members; (ii) accept the resignation of one existing member of the Board to become effective immediately prior to the appointment of New Director A; and (iii) appoint New Director A to the Board with a term on the Board expiring at the Company's 2018 Annual Meeting of Stockholders (the "2018 Annual Meeting") and until his successor is duly elected and qualified.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Company" to "Organization" in Section 1(a) creates confusion because the term "Organization" is not defined anywhere, while "Company" is defined as “The Meet Group, Inc., a Delaware corporation”. This creates uncertainty about who is responsible for the actions described, directly contradicting with the full section 1 below, because it is unclear if it is reffering to "The Meet Group, Inc., a Delaware corporation".
perturbation.contradicted_text: Appointment of New Director A. The Organization agrees that it shall take all action as is necessary (including, without limitation, calling a special meeting of the Board to approve all actions contemplated hereby), effective immediately following the execution of this Agreement, to (i) cause the Board to increase the size of its membership from six (6) to seven (7) members; (ii) accept the resignation of one existing member of the Board to become effective immediately prior to the appointment of New Director A; and (iii) appoint New Director A to the Board with a term on the Board expiring at the Company's 2018 Annual Meeting of Stockholders (the "2018 Annual Meeting") and until his successor is duly elected and qualified.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Company" to "Organization" in Section 1(a) introduces ambiguity. The original text defines "Company" as “The Meet Group, Inc., a Delaware corporation”. By using "Organization" instead, the contract creates uncertainty as to which entity is obligated to perform the actions described in that section, directly contradicting the intent and clarity of the original clause regarding Appointment of New Director A. The contradicted text is also Section 1(a), where 'Organization' appears again, highlighting the in-text contradiction. This misalignment in terminology, as defined, causes the in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (d) Board Policies and Procedures. The Investor Group acknowledges that each of the New Directors shall be required to comply with all policies, processes, procedures, codes, rules, standards, and guidelines applicable to members of the Board, as in effect from time to time, including, but not limited to, the Company's Code of Conduct, and policies on confidentiality, ethics, hedging and pledging of Company securities, public disclosures, stock trading, and stock ownership, and that each of the New Directors shall be required to strictly preserve the confidentiality of Company business and information, including the discussion of any matters considered in meetings of the Board whether or not the matters relate to material non-public information, unless previously publicly disclosed by the Company. Further, the Investor Group acknowledges that the New Directors will be requested to provide the Company with such information as is reasonably requested by the Company concerning the New Directors as is required to be disclosed under applicable law or stock exchange regulations, including the completion of the Company's standard director and officer questionnaire, in each case as promptly as necessary to enable the timely filing of the Company's proxy statement and other periodic reports with the SEC.
perturbation.changed_text: (d) Board Policies and Procedures. The Investor Group acknowledges that each of the New Directors shall be required to comply with all policies, processes, procedures, codes, rules, standards, and guidelines applicable to members of the Board, as in effect from time to time, including, but not limited to, the Enterprise's Code of Conduct, and policies on confidentiality, ethics, hedging and pledging of Enterprise securities, public disclosures, stock trading, and stock ownership, and that each of the New Directors shall be required to strictly preserve the confidentiality of Enterprise business and information, including the discussion of any matters considered in meetings of the Board whether or not the matters relate to material non-public information, unless previously publicly disclosed by the Enterprise. Further, the Investor Group acknowledges that the New Directors will be requested to provide the Enterprise with such information as is reasonably requested by the Enterprise concerning the New Directors as is required to be disclosed under applicable law or stock exchange regulations, including the completion of the Company's standard director and officer questionnaire, in each case as promptly as necessary to enable the timely filing of the Company's proxy statement and other periodic reports with the SEC.
perturbation.explanation: In section 1(d), the term "Company" is replaced by "Enterprise" in the first part of the section, but not in the second part where it says: "including the completion of the Company's standard director and officer questionnaire, in each case as promptly as necessary to enable the timely filing of the Company's proxy statement and other periodic reports with the SEC." This creates an internal contradiction, because it is uncertain on what code of conduct they should follow, which questionnaire should be filled, which securities should be considered for the hedging and pledging policies and also to what business the confidentiality is been reffering.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Board Policies and Procedures. The Investor Group acknowledges that each of the New Directors shall be required to comply with all policies, processes, procedures, codes, rules, standards, and guidelines applicable to members of the Board, as in effect from time to time, including, but not limited to, the Enterprise's Code of Conduct, and policies on confidentiality, ethics, hedging and pledging of Enterprise securities, public disclosures, stock trading, and stock ownership, and that each of the New Directors shall be required to strictly preserve the confidentiality of Enterprise business and information, including the discussion of any matters considered in meetings of the Board whether or not the matters relate to material non-public information, unless previously publicly disclosed by the Enterprise. Further, the Investor Group acknowledges that the New Directors will be requested to provide the Enterprise with such information as is reasonably requested by the Company concerning the New Directors as is required to be disclosed under applicable law or stock exchange regulations, including the completion of the Company's standard director and officer questionnaire, in each case as promptly as necessary to enable the timely filing of the Company's proxy statement and other periodic reports with the SEC.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Company' with 'Enterprise' in most of section 1(d), except for the phrase 'Company's standard director and officer questionnaire,' which remains unchanged. This creates a clear contradiction because it's unclear whether the New Directors should complete the 'Company's' questionnaire or the 'Enterprise's' questionnaire. It also raises questions about which entity's proxy statement and periodic reports are relevant, creating ambiguity and potential non-compliance. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the inconsistent use of 'Company' and 'Enterprise' leads to conflicting obligations and makes enforcement unclear, as the contradicted text also include the mentioned changes.

perturbation.original_text: Each Investor agrees that, from the date of this Agreement until the expiration of the Standstill Period (as defined below), without the prior written consent of a majority of the Board specifically expressed in a written resolution, neither it nor any of its Related Persons (as defined herein) will, and it will cause each of its Related Persons not to, directly or indirectly, alone or with others, in any manner: (i) propose or publicly announce or otherwise publicly disclose an intent to propose or enter into or agree to enter into, singly or with any other person, directly or indirectly, (x) any form of business combination or acquisition or other transaction relating to a material amount of assets or securities of the Company or any of its subsidiaries, (y) any form of restructuring, recapitalization or similar transaction with respect to the Company or any of its subsidiaries or (z) any form of tender or exchange offer for the Common Stock, whether or not such transaction involves a change of control of the Company;
perturbation.changed_text: Each Investor agrees that, from the date of this Agreement until the expiration of the Standstill Period (as defined below), without the prior written consent of a majority of the Board specifically expressed in a written resolution, neither it nor any of its Related Persons (as defined herein) will, and it will cause each of its Related Persons not to, directly or indirectly, alone or with others, in any manner: (i) propose or publicly announce or otherwise publicly disclose an intent to propose or enter into or agree to enter into, singly or with any other person, directly or indirectly, (x) any form of business combination or acquisition or other transaction relating to a material amount of assets or securities of the Corporation or any of its subsidiaries, (y) any form of restructuring, recapitalization or similar transaction with respect to the Company or any of its subsidiaries or (z) any form of tender or exchange offer for the Common Stock, whether or not such transaction involves a change of control of the Company;
perturbation.explanation: In Section 3(a)(i), "Company" is changed to "Corporation" in subsection (x) but remains "Company" in subsections (y) and (z). This creates an internal inconsistency about whose assets and control are being restricted, because it is uncertain which company they are reffering to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: propose or publicly announce or otherwise publicly disclose an intent to propose or enter into or agree to enter into, singly or with any other person, directly or indirectly, (x) any form of business combination or acquisition or other transaction relating to a material amount of assets or securities of the Corporation or any of its subsidiaries, (y) any form of restructuring, recapitalization or similar transaction with respect to the Company or any of its subsidiaries or (z) any form of tender or exchange offer for the Common Stock, whether or not such transaction involves a change of control of the Company;
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Company' with 'Corporation' in subsection (x) while maintaining 'Company' in subsections (y) and (z). This introduces a clear inconsistency in the definition of the entity whose assets and control are subject to the restrictions outlined in Section 3(a)(i). The contradiction arises from the use of both terms, 'Company' and 'Corporation,' within the same clause, leading to ambiguity and uncertainty about which entity is being referred to. This ambiguity directly impacts the enforceability and interpretation of the standstill agreement, creating a direct conflict within the document, as described in the Perturbation Explanation. This falls under the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction because the terms are used interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.
